name: Percy, H. All.
motto: The man that hath no music in himself and is not moved with concord of sweet sounds is fit for treasons, strategems and spoils.
description: He was our adjutant and published the glad tidings at retreat.

name: Joseph, W. Anderson
motto: And I pray you let none of your people stir me; I have an exposition of sleep come upon me.
description: Was a student of a quiet disposition and many accomplishments.

name: James, W. Blease.
motto: Be true to your word and your work and your friend.
clubs: Vice President and President Columbian Society
description: Jim was the best of students, and had a noble disposition.

name: William, A. Burgess
motto: The man who is fond of books is usually a man of lofty thought and of elevated opinions.
description: Was a "D. D. C.," but one of the best sort.

name: Asheley, A. Butler
motto: Hope against hope, and ask till ye receive.
description: Sergeant '00. A more persevering student could not be found than was Ashley.

name: Harry, L. Cannon
motto: O sleep, why dost thou leave me? Why thy visionary joys remove?
description: We predict a brilliant future for this our modern gun.

name: J. Cheatham, Earnest
motto: Men possessed with an idea cannot be reasoned with.
description: He was a man of great military ability.

name: William, R. Darlington
motto: Let me have audience for a word or two.
description: All we can say of him is "That the half has not yet been told."

name: Joseph, C. Duckworth
motto: Every thought which genius and piety throw into the world, alters the world.
clubs: Football Team, sub '97, left guard '98, left guard '99, left tackle '00
description: He reminds one of Socrates.

name: Warren, N. Fair
motto: True as the needle to the pole, or the dial to the sun.
description: Enjoys the friendship of others; his jolly nature makes him liked by all who know him.

name: R. Forsythe, Graham
motto: A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays and confident to-morrow.
description: With his brush he shows a master's hand.

name: T. Knox, Glenn.
motto: Innocence in genius, and candor in power, are both noble qualities.
description: "Knox" was a "D.D.C," but as modest as you please.

name: W. G. Hill
motto: Be wise with speed; A fool at forty is a fool indeed.
clubs: Second Honor Medal, State Oratorical Contest 1900
description: He takes well with the ladies.

name: Eddings, T. Hughes.
motto: Let fame that all hunt after in their lives Live registered upon our brazen tombs.
description: "Ed" is an eloquent speaker yet he did not put pebbles in his mouth.

name: John, G. Kaigler
motto: None but himself can be his parallel.
description: It is a great treat to be in his company.

name: Klugh, G. Fred
motto: He only is a well made man who has a good determination.
description: His roommates agree that he is good in an argument of any kind.

name: Thomas Oregon, Lawton
motto: To know, to esteem, to love—and then to part, Makes up life's tale to many a feeling heart.
description: "Boy" had a kindly feeling in his heart for everyone.

name: Lewis, J. Baxter
motto: The deed I intend is great; but what, as yet, I know not.
clubs: Class Historian Business Manager Clemson's Football '00
description: He is not noted for his military genius, but his light shines in different spheres.

name: H. Murray, Mathis
motto: He wears the roses of youth upon him.
description: Although the youngest of our class, he possesses many talents.

name: W. E. McLendon
motto: He thought as a sage, though he felt as a man.
description: Will probably devote himself to the study of Chemistry. "Mc." hopes to rival Fressenius.

name: William, H. Newell
motto: Everywhere in life the true question is, not what we gain, but what we do.

name: William, A. Sanders
motto: Genius must be born, and never can be taught.
description: Space will not permit me to write his biography; yet it should be written and preserved in a library.

name: Walter, H. Scott
motto: Some are born great, some achieve greatness, and some have greatness thrust upon them.
description: We wish to make of him a mill president.

name: Hugh, R. Tison
motto: We must laugh before we are happy for fear we may die before we laugh at all.
description: "Monk" is the funniest boy you ever saw - his mere presence was sufficient to dispel the blues. Was a scholarly lad besides. He was a good student and will win his way to the front.

name: Quincy, B. Newman
motto: We know what we are; but know not what we may be.
clubs: President South Carolina Inter-Collegiate Oratorical Association
description: His face is familiar to everyone.

name: Ernest, H. Pickett
motto: I dare do all that may become a man, Who dares do more, is none.
description: He was a student of pluck and good judgment.

name: Hollis, L. Ramsey
motto: Man is man and master of his fate.
description: A somewhat modest student.

name: Robert, N. Reeves
motto: He is in deep sympathy with mankind, also womankind.
clubs: Treasurer and Vice President Calhoun Society
description: He is fond of Military life.

name: J. H. Roddey
motto: I awoke one morning and found myself famous.
description: His history is not yet completed, which means that we shall have to wait for it.

name: James, E. Sally.
motto: The mould of a man's fortune is in his own hands.
description: Having made a fine record while at college, we expect him to win equal success in the battles of life.

name: Edgar, M. Matthews
motto: The good of others he made his chief concern.
